epics.title: add-epic --link accepts malformed format silently; update-epic has no --link flag to fix it
Epic-hierarchy orchestration — run a parent epic's children unattended (see docs/vision/2026-07-14-autonomy-orchestration-vision.md, section B)
Portfolio/architecture-consistency scanning across the backlog (see docs/vision/2026-07-14-autonomy-orchestration-vision.md, section C — least-scoped of the three, needs its own brainstorm)

links.reason: C presumes B's hierarchy execution exists to propagate cross-cutting decisions into

epics.title: Wire infra-runbook preflight (~/Servers/infra-playbooks) into pm's epic/hierarchy preflight (see docs/vision/2026-07-14-autonomy-orchestration-vision.md, section D — cheapest of the three, existing convention just needs wiring)
pm plugin improvements from three dogfooding sessions (2026-07-14) — see docs/feedback/2026-07-14-pm-plugin-improvement-feedback.md
Flip gate-guard default to ON for epics with reconcileOnResume — reverses the deliberate opt-in choice, approved after real-usage feedback showed it's never been turned on

preAuthorized.action: change gate-guard default computation to treat reconcileNeeded
preAuthorized.reason: true as gate-guard-on regardless of the repo's explicit gateGuard setting, retroactively:matches recorded decision

context: Decision: RETROACTIVE — flip applies to any epic with reconcileNeeded:true right now, not just new paused frames going forward. Accepted risk: an in-flight session could be surprised by writes suddenly blocked.

epics.title: Auto-detect an unlogged minimal detour from commit/diff shape and log it without a manual /pm:detour --minimal invocation — 3 sessions converge on this, user's #1 priority

preAuthorized.action: add a new PostToolUse hook (or extend the existing commit-nudge hook) that runs a diff-shape heuristic and auto-logs a minimal detour

context: Decision: AUTOMATIC PostToolUse hook on every commit (not on-demand) — highest-payoff per 3-session convergence on this as the #1 pain point. Heuristic TBD at build time (file count / line count / new-OpenSpec-proposal presence) but the trigger mechanism itself is settled: hook-driven, not agent-remembered.

epics.title: Add a remove-epic (or soft-delete/archive-with-reason) CLI verb — the only current recovery from a mis-registered epic is a raw git checkout

preAuthorized.action: hard-delete an epic with zero children and strip any dangling links referencing it, printing a warning
block (not silently allow) removal of an epic that still has children, surfacing a table for human review

context: Decisions from preflight Q&A: (1) hard delete (splice from array) — recoverable only via git history, matching the git-checkout workaround this replaces; (2) dangling links[] in OTHER epics referencing the removed id are stripped automatically and a warning naming affected epics is printed; (3) removal of an epic with children is BLOCKED BY DEFAULT — prints a concise table (id, title, short summary) of the parent + all children — with a --cascade flag as an explicit escape hatch to remove the parent and all descendants in one go; the agent must surface this table and get explicit human confirmation before ever passing --cascade, since the engine itself has no interactive prompt

stories.title: Preflight scan + Q&A recorded via set-autonomy
TDD: remove-epic hard-delete, --cascade, link-stripping, active-clear tests

epics.title: Fix missing() to exclude status===archived — an already-closed epic can show an unresolvable '⚠ no change on disk' warning forever

context: Preflight: no destructive risk, no genuine unknown — exclude status===archived from missing()'s condition, a direct bug fix.

epics.title: Reconciler returns a structured {valid, amendments} verdict AND writes it back onto the relevant link — currently the judgment only ever lives in conversation

preAuthorized.action: add reconciledAt/verdict/amendments fields to a link object during POP reconcile
preAuthorized.reason: matches default schema, additive field, no existing data removed

context: Preflight: writes a structured verdict onto the relevant link (state.json mutation, git-recoverable, low risk). Default schema absent user override: {verdict: 'valid'|'invalidated', amendments: string[], reconciledAt: iso-timestamp} attached to the link object.

epics.title: Add a real per-repo lane-routing override mechanism (state.json config) instead of requiring a CLAUDE.md prose carve-out when the generic heuristic is wrong for a repo

context: Preflight: adds a new optional state.json config block (e.g. laneRouting: {overrides: [...]}), no destructive risk. Default absent user override: array of {match: <glob or keyword>, lane: <lane>} rules checked before the generic heuristic.

epics.title: Investigate why epic-level autonomy and epic-hierarchy orchestration show zero real-world usage in at least one dogfooding repo — ceremony weight vs discoverability vs genuinely not needed

context: Preflight: read-only investigation (grep git log/detours.log for set-autonomy/hierarchy usage), no destructive risk, no code changes.

epics.title: log-detour/resume print the exact ready-to-copy Honcho memory string (and keep a local log of what was emitted) instead of asking the agent to compose and silently one-way-write it

context: Preflight: pure string formatting + local log file (e.g. .conductor/honcho-memories.log), no network call (law-compliant), no destructive risk.

epics.title: Add a verify-state command that fails loudly if state.json's mtime is newer than the last render's stamp, catching an undetected hand-edit mechanically

preAuthorized.action: add a renderedAt stamp written by render() and a verify-state command reading it
preAuthorized.reason: additive schema field, no data removed

context: Preflight: read-only check comparing state.json mtime vs a last-render stamp; needs a new stamp field/file written by render() to compare against — additive, no destructive risk.

epics.title: Extend plan-hierarchy's depends-on topological sort to top-level queued epics generally, not just parent/child hierarchies — the same starvation problem exists at the top-level queue

preAuthorized.action: extend plan-hierarchy's existing topological-sort helper to top-level queued epics generally, and change /pm
preAuthorized.reason: next's selection + brief's stated reason:matches recorded decision, no data mutation, pure selection-logic change

context: Decision: BEHAVIORAL — /pm:next must SKIP a starved epic whose depends-on target is still queued/unresolved, even if the starved epic is higher priority. ADDITIONALLY: when /pm:next (or the brief) picks an epic that only won because a higher-priority sibling is blocked on a dependency, it must print a concise one-line reason naming the blocking epic, not just silently pick.

epics.title: Let a single epic/detour override set-review-mode's repo-global dial to thorough without flipping the whole repo's mode

context: Preflight: no destructive risk. Default absent user override: an epic-level override can only ESCALATE (e.g. force thorough on a standard-mode repo), never silently de-escalate below the repo's dial.

epics.title: Add startedAt/completedAt timestamps to epics, and surface a staleness/days-active indicator in PROJECT.md and /pm:next — supports velocity tracking and the weekly Ship-Real-Software check

context: Preflight: adds startedAt/completedAt fields (additive, no destructive risk). Default staleness threshold absent user override: 14 days since startedAt with no completedAt = stale flag in PROJECT.md/brief.

epics.title: Add a category-based --preauthorize shorthand (e.g. "category:filesystem") to epic-level autonomy, instead of requiring every routine action to be enumerated individually

context: Preflight: no destructive risk (additive autonomy shorthand). Default category taxonomy absent user override: filesystem, network, schema, external-api — a --preauthorize 'category:filesystem:...' expands to match any action tagged with that category by the decision rule.

epics.title: Clarify the epic-level-autonomy decision rule's wording so WARN-class decisions are --notify'd incrementally as they happen, not just gathered for the end-of-epic report — a compaction-safety fix using the mechanism that already exists

context: Preflight: no destructive risk, no genuine unknown — pure wording fix in the decision-rule doc/skill text: 'record incrementally as it happens' instead of 'record for the end-of-epic report'.

epics.title: Investigate a mechanism for cross-repo epic dependencies — links currently only reference epics within one repo, but real cross-repo dependencies exist (e.g. a deploy epic depending on infra-playbooks conventions)

context: Preflight: read-only investigation/design-question gathering, no destructive risk, no code changes — deliverable is a scoped writeup, not a fix.

epics.title: Show the top 2-3 changelog bullets inline in the SessionStart upgrade nudge, instead of requiring a separate /pm:changelog round trip to decide if upgrading is worth mid-epic churn

context: Preflight: no destructive risk (display-only). Default scope absent user override: inline just the 'Added' bullet headlines (not full body) for versions between stamped and newest, to avoid bloating every SessionStart hook output.

epics.title: Skill-driven /pm:feedback command — post a bug report or feature request as a GitHub issue on cfdude/pm directly from any Claude Code session using the plugin

GitHub issue tracker: extend set-tracker/sync so open cfdude/pm issues can be pulled into the conductor as untriaged epics, same pattern as OpenSpec/Superpowers auto-registration — closes the loop with ai-feedback-loop-github-issues

links.reason: issues created by the feedback loop are exactly what this syncs back in

epics.title: Secure and automate the cfdude/pm GitHub repo: branch protection, CI, and (eventually) autonomous issue-to-PR resolution
Protect main on cfdude/pm: require PRs into main (no direct pushes), operate off a dev branch, matching the ff-only dev/main convention already used elsewhere this session
Add a GitHub Actions workflow on cfdude/pm running node --test (and syntax/lint checks) on every PR — CI gate before merge

Research + design an autonomous issue-to-PR pipeline for cfdude/pm: a GitHub-Actions-hosted Claude agent that analyzes a validated issue, implements a fix on a branch, and opens a PR; a CI check gates that the PR genuinely came from this trusted automation (not a spoofed external PR); the user remains the final human approver/merger always. Needs its own research pass (what mechanism — an official Claude Code GitHub Action, a custom Claude API workflow, something else) before any design — substantially bigger and more novel than the other two governance items.

DF: conductor skill's $ENGINE resolution silently falls back to the installed plugin CACHE with a confusing generic 'usage:' error and no version-mismatch signal, when developing pm on itself. Hit live while dogfood-testing remove-epic: ran the documented ENGINE-resolution snippet against the 0.10.0 cached copy (predates this feature) and got the same bare usage-line a typo'd subcommand would produce, zero indication a newer engine existed one directory up in the repo being edited; only found the cause by knowingly invoking scripts/conductor.mjs directly. Any self-hosting dogfood session testing conductor.mjs changes live via the skill's own snippet hits this same silent trap. Fix ideas: prefer a conductor.mjs at CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts over the plugin cache when it exists (repo IS the plugin source); or print engine version+source path on every invocation so staleness is visible instead of surfacing as an opaque usage error

preAuthorized.action: edit ENGINE-resolution logic in conductor skill docs + any engine self-print addition
preAuthorized.reason: matches recorded decision, additive/observability only, no data risk

context: Decision: BOTH — (1) prefer repo-local scripts/conductor.mjs over plugins cache when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/conductor.mjs exists (self-hosting case); (2) every invocation prints engine version+source path to stderr so staleness is visible

epics.title: DF: plan-hierarchy includes an already-archived child epic in its batch output instead of filtering it out — found live running plan-hierarchy --parent pm-plugin-improvements-2026-07-14 after archiving remove-epic-verb: it still appeared in batch 0 with autonomous:true, dependsOn:[], indistinguishable from real not-yet-done work. An agent following the documented dispatch process ('dispatch one hierarchy-child-executor per epic in the batch') would blindly re-dispatch already-completed work unless it manually cross-checks status first. Fix: plan-hierarchy's batch computation should exclude status===archived children (same class of bug as fix-archived-no-change-warning, different code path)

DF: plan-hierarchy's all-parallel batch (16 siblings, no depends-on edges) has no notion that many children touch the SAME source file — every pm-plugin-improvements-2026-07-14 child modifies scripts/conductor.mjs, so a literal 'dispatch every epic in this batch in parallel' per the conductor skill's documented process would race N concurrent Edit/Write calls against one file. Found while about to dispatch the real 17-child batch for this session's hierarchy test — priority/depends-on scheduling alone doesn't capture this hazard; plan-hierarchy has no file/path overlap signal at all. Needs its own design (e.g. an optional touchedPaths hint per epic feeding batch computation, or per-batch worktree isolation the orchestrator forces automatically when it can't prove non-overlap)
